discovery.run_summary: Semi-cap equipment names (VECO, ONTO, AEIS, LSCC) getting hammered as SMH drops sharply, while defense (AVAV, KTOS) rips on continued sector rerating. Broad tape mixed with rotation out of tech into defensives (XLV, XLP, XLU all up 2%+) as weak jobs report drives risk-off in growth but risk-on in rate-sensitive sectors.

notable_index_moves: SMH -4.54% is the standout — semi-equipment names cratering with it
XLV +2.63%, XLP +2.03%, XLU +2.21% — classic defensive rotation on weak payrolls (+57k vs +115k expected)
TSLA -7.49% despite strong deliveries; META -4.9%; NVDA -1.4% — mega-cap tech under pressure
ITA +1.78%, defense tape strong on Quantum Systems $1.2B raise headline

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings continuation rally into an extended multi-day run
discoveries.thesis: AVAV reported earnings 6/29 (2.02 + 7.01 8-K) and has since had a substantial multi-day extension. Today's additional gain layered onto an already large 5-day move plus a hot defense-tech tape (Quantum Systems $1.2B raise) looks like late-stage momentum chasing. Earnings was clearly good, but the magnitude of the multi-day move likely overshoots fundamentals as retail piles into the defense-drone narrative.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side price targets lag the current price by a wide margin; insider Form 4 selling appears in coming days; drone-sector ETFs show signs of blow-off volume
discoveries.what_kills: New DoD contract announcement or specific guidance raise embedded in the 7.01 FD disclosure that justifies a step-change in revenue outlook
discoveries.what_to_learn: Post-earnings drift in hot-sector defense names frequently overshoots by 15-20% before fading; look for the 7.01 FD disclosure content to distinguish real guide-up from generic investor deck
discoveries.catalyst: 6/29 earnings 8-K (2.02 + 7.01) combined with sector-wide defense tech enthusiasm
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K contains results (2.02) plus a Reg FD disclosure (7.01) — the FD item suggests management shared forward-looking color, which explains follow-through, but the extended 5-day move suggests the market has more than priced it in
research_pointers: Read the 7.01 exhibit for actual guidance language vs analyst expectations
Compare current EV/sales to KTOS and pure-play drone peers
Check short interest and options skew for signs of squeeze exhaustion
Look for defense-ETF inflow data as sentiment proxy

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap equipment sympathy selloff with no company-specific 8-K
discoveries.thesis: VECO down sharply with no filed catalyst, but SMH -4.5% and peer semi-equipment names (ONTO, AEIS) down double digits too — this is sector-wide capex-cycle repricing, not company-specific. Small-cap semi-cap has higher beta to the group, so the outsized move is proportionate. Not obviously mispriced either direction without more color on what triggered the SMH move.
discoveries.what_confirms: Peer AEIS/ONTO release cautious commentary; a major foundry defers capex; SOX index breaks key support
discoveries.what_kills: Isolated company-specific bad news emerges (customer loss, guide cut) that the peer selloff doesn't explain
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No 8-K filings; three semi-cap names down 6-18% together suggests group move, not idiosyncratic
research_pointers: Identify the trigger for today's SMH decline (any specific customer capex cut news?)
Check VECO's exposure to memory vs logic capex
Review recent semi-cap guidance from LRCX, AMAT for read-across

discoveries.name: Lattice Semiconductor Corporation
discoveries.setup: 8-K with material agreement plus debt obligation on the same day as sector selloff
discoveries.thesis: LSCC filed a 1.01 (material definitive agreement) + 2.03 (new financial obligation) 8-K on 6/30. The combination typically signals a leveraged transaction — could be an acquisition or refinancing. Stock is down with the semi tape but the presence of a real filed catalyst that markets may not have fully digested (given noise from SMH -4.5%) suggests the specific-company component is being under-priced relative to the sector-driven move. Direction depends on the deal terms, but the co-filing of new debt with a material agreement typically indicates the market needs to reprice capital structure — usually bearish short-term until clarity.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibit reveals a debt-funded acquisition at rich valuation or covenant terms that squeeze equity; downgrade from a covering analyst on leverage grounds
discoveries.what_kills: The agreement turns out to be a favorable customer contract with a routine credit facility resize, in which case the sector-noise selloff creates a buying opportunity
discoveries.what_to_learn: 1.01 + 2.03 combinations should always be read before assuming sympathy explains the move — new debt obligations filed alongside material agreements almost always warrant a repricing beyond what sector beta explains
discoveries.catalyst: 6/30 8-K filing with material agreement and new financial obligation
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Co-filed 1.01 and 2.03 on 6/30 — this pattern typically indicates a debt-financed transaction; magnitude of down-move blends with the SMH selloff, obscuring the specific catalyst
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibit list for the actual agreement (M&A target, debt facility terms)
Estimate incremental leverage and interest cost vs current cash generation
Check if any analyst has published on the deal specifics vs sector-only commentary

discoveries.name: National Beverage Corp.
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings gap on low float defensive name
discoveries.thesis: Reported earnings 7/1 and stock is up sharply on 2.6x volume. FIZZ is a low-float, controlled-company beverage name (LaCroix) known for volatile reactions. Without the actual earnings numbers vs consensus, and with the stock net flat over 5 days despite today's pop, it's not clear whether this is a legitimate beat, a short squeeze, or a mean-reversion bounce from a pre-earnings drawdown.
discoveries.what_confirms: N/A — need actual earnings data to form a directional view
discoveries.what_kills: N/A — need actual earnings data to form a directional view
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: recent_earnings signal confirms print; no 8-K text available in feed to assess beat/miss magnitude
research_pointers: Pull the actual EPS/revenue vs consensus
Check LaCroix volume trends and any commentary on private-label competition
Assess whether pop is retracing a pre-earnings sell-off or breaking to new highs

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
discoveries.setup: Sympathy rally with AVAV on defense-tech momentum
discoveries.thesis: KTOS up notably on top of a large 5-day gain, no 8-K catalyst, riding the defense-drone theme (AVAV earnings, Quantum Systems $1.2B raise). The 5-day extension without company-specific news suggests momentum chasing that may exhaust when the AVAV catalyst is fully digested. Lower conviction than AVAV itself because Kratos has genuine tailwind from budget flows, so the sector bid may persist longer than a pure sympathy call assumes.
discoveries.what_confirms: AVAV starts to fade; ITA ETF stalls; no company-specific KTOS contract announcement lands
discoveries.what_kills: KTOS-specific DoD contract award or investor day guidance raise
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with AVAV earnings and defense-tech capital raises
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No filings; move coincides with AVAV +10% and defense sector strength
research_pointers: Check for any pending KTOS contract announcements or DoD budget line items
Compare current KTOS valuation multiple to 5-year range
Monitor ITA ETF flows for signs of theme exhaustion

catalyst_chains.primary_event: SMH down 4.5% dragging semi-cap equipment names lower
AVAV earnings and Quantum Systems $1.2B raise reinforcing defense-drone theme

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump touting falling oil/gas prices, announcing 'Freedom Fuel Network' 25-station discount promo in Philadelphia for July 3
trump_signals.note: Rhetorical continuation of the pressure-lower-oil narrative; no direct policy change. XLE actually up 0.78% today, suggesting market ignoring the jawboning. Low signal value for individual mid-caps.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic in talks with Samsung for custom AI chip, following OpenAI/Broadcom announcement last week. Signals continued trend of hyperscaler AI labs moving toward custom silicon.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco supplies deposition/etch equipment used broadly in advanced logic and HBM manufacturing; custom chips from Samsung would still require its tools. More custom silicon = more foundry capex, potentially net positive.
research_pointers: Check if VECO had a specific negative catalyst today (guide-down, analyst downgrade) — -18% with only 1.34x volume and no 8-K suggests company-specific news outside this news set
Verify Samsung foundry exposure in Veeco revenue mix
5-day -23.8% suggests multi-day slide, not single AI-news reaction

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Onto's metrology/inspection tools are essential for advanced packaging (HBM, CoWoS) — the exact bottleneck for AI chips including any Anthropic/Samsung custom design. Custom silicon proliferation is structurally bullish.
research_pointers: Likely a sympathy move with VECO or a semi-cap sector event not captured in this news feed
No 8-K signal — look for peer guide-down (KLAC, AMAT, LRCX) or WFE demand commentary
HBM/advanced packaging exposure remains structurally strong

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Advanced Energy sells power delivery to semi-cap OEMs and data centers — both segments benefit from custom AI silicon buildout.
research_pointers: Semi-cap sector sell-off pattern (VECO/ONTO/AEIS all down double digits) — investigate common catalyst
5-day -17% consistent with sector-wide de-rating, not Anthropic-specific
AI chip news should be neutral-to-positive for AEIS long-term

ai_announcements.headline: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5B commitment
ai_announcements.summary: Microsoft joins Amazon, OpenAI, Anthropic in standing up dedicated AI deployment arm.

ai_announcements.headline: Indian tycoon bets $30M on AI alternative to Microsoft Office
ai_announcements.summary: Neo, a new AI-native productivity suite challenger to MSFT Office and Google Workspace.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Semi-cap equipment names (VECO -18%, ONTO -12%, AEIS -13%) are down hard but none of today's AI news items plausibly explains the magnitude. More likely a sector-specific catalyst (peer guide, China export rule, analyst downgrade) not in this news feed. AVAV/KTOS defense moves and CRSP/FIZZ/DECK are unrelated to AI announcements. Flagging Anthropic-related call: leaning consensus-market on the custom chip story — it is not directly bearish for semi-cap tools.
